rationale: The cited paper is highly topically aligned with the citing paper’s core mechanism: both explicitly study co-occurring assimilation and contrast in hedonic judgments (citing focal constructs include “hedonic contrast/assimilation,” while cited contribution is “multiple reference points” producing both effects). Given the citing paper’s claim that it refines prior hedonic-contrast interpretations, this 2014 JMR article is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental background mention.
